body: PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (AADI) is seeking to raise IDR 4.32 trillion through its initial public offering, with 37.23% of the proceeds allocated to loan its subsidiary, PT Maritim Barito Perkasa, and another portion for refinancing loans with PT Adaro Indonesia and PT Alamtri Resources Indonesia Tbk. The company will pay back IDR 635.52 billion (USD 40 million) of a USD 50 million facility and IDR 2.04 trillion of a USD 1.45 billion facility, with interest rates ranging from 1.2% to 1.4% per annum.
title: Adaro Andalan Indonesia targets to raise IDR 4.32 trillion from IPO to fund subsidiary loan and refinance existing debts.

body: Nineteen bonds from 15 issuers, including PT Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Tbk (INKP), PT Steel Pipe Industry Indonesia Tbk (SPINDO), and PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure Tbk, are set to mature this year, with a total debt payment of IDR 6.43 trillion. The largest bond debt payment will be made by PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure Tbk, totaling IDR 1.51 trillion, followed by PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Jawa Barat dan Banten Tbk's IDR 1.47 billion payment.
title: Nineteen bonds from fifteen issuers in Indonesia are set to mature by the end of the year, with payments totaling billions of rupiah.

body: Two members of PT Maskapai Reasuransi Indonesia Tbk (MREI)'s board of commissioners, Robby Loho and Sarkoro Handajani, submitted their resignations, reportedly related to an internal rotation plan, with their departures pending a fit and proper test from the Financial Services Authority. Meanwhile, MREI's stock price rose IDR 10 to IDR 985 per share by 12 pm WIB, a positive shift since the opening bell.
title: Two PT Maskapai Reasuransi Indonesia Board Commissioners Submit Resignations Amidst Internal Rotation Plan

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) won the "Most Trusted Company" award at the 2024 Corporate Governance Perception Index Award, recognizing its commitment to good corporate governance and sustainable performance, with a solid financial performance maintained despite global macroeconomic challenges. BNI's risk management and cyber security practices, including its risk maturity index assessment and national cyber and crypto agency evaluation, have contributed to its success in mitigating negative impacts from external factors.
title: BNI Wins Most Trusted Company Award at 2024 Corporate Governance Perception Index for Its Commitment to Good Corporate Governance.

body: PT Karya Pacific Investama acquired a 24.91% stake in PT MNC Energy Investments TBK (IATA) by purchasing 6.28 billion shares, while PT MNC Asia Holding TBK (MNC) reduced its stake from 44.08% to 30% by releasing 3.55 billion shares. Meanwhile, various investors made notable transactions, including purchases in PT Wintermar Offshore Marine TBK (WINS), PT Medikaloka Hermina TBK (HEAL), and PT Tempo Scan Pacific TBK (TKIM), and sales in PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK (NSS) and PT Bank JTrust Indonesia TBK (BJTI).
title: Multiple investors make significant share purchases and sales in various Indonesian companies, including energy, healthcare, and finance sectors.

body: PT Perusahaan Perkebunan London Sumatra Tbk (LSIP) shares fell 14.23% to IDR 1,055 per share last month, but foreign investors have been consistently buying up shares, with a total net foreign buy of 45.03 million shares since the beginning of the year. Despite stagnant revenue growth, LSIP's net profit reached IDR 803 billion in 9M 2024, up 76% year-on-year, driven by a 94% contribution from palm oil products to its total revenue.
title: Foreign investors snap up shares of PT Perusahaan Perkebunan London Sumatra despite recent price drop.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 254.17 million shares, with Digital Corporation PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) leading the way with 314.80 million shares, followed by PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ASTR) with 230.38 million shares. On the other hand, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares, including 213.25 million shares of PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and 106.76 million shares of PT Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA).
title: Foreign investors purchase 4.39 billion shares in Indonesian market, with net surplus of 254.17 million shares.

body: PT Majukarya Mandiri Indonesia (MMI) has increased its stake in PT Sumber Energi Andalan TBK (ITMA) to 7.03% following a recent transaction, up from 5.69% as of November 22. As of November 26, ITMA's issued and paid-up capital stands at 999.05 million shares, with PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK holding 41.89%, Zurich Asset International Ltd holding 13.36%, and the public holding 37.71%.
title: PT Majukarya Mandiri Indonesia increases stake in PT Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk to 7.03%.

body: Foreign investors have been divesting their shares in PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA), with a net sell of 8.88 million shares on Tuesday, 590.17 million shares on Monday, and 17.61 million shares on Friday, making SCMA the top company with the highest foreign net sell over the past three days. As a result, SCMA's share price has dropped 5.11% or 7 points to IDR 130 per share, and has plunged 15.03% or 23 points since the beginning of the year.
title: Foreign investors sell off PT Surya Citra Media shares, driving net sell to highest level in three days.

body: Sugiman Layanto increased his stake in PT Wintermar Offshore Marine Tbk (WINS) to 7.60% from 7.55% by acquiring 2.4 million shares at an average price of IDR 461.5 per share, totaling IDR 1.10 billion. With this transaction, Layanto becomes the President Director and controlling shareholder of WINS, which had a total of 4.36 billion shares and a shareholder structure of 41.57% controlling party and 58.42% non-controllers as of October 2024.
title: Sugiman Layanto increases stake in PT Wintermar Offshore Marine to 7.60% through 2.4 million share acquisition.

body: Foreign investors have been divesting shares of PT Alamatri Resources Indonesia Tbk (ADRO), a coal mining company, with a net sell of 152.96 million shares in the last 7 days, contributing to a 24.80% decline in its share price to IDR 2,760 per share. Despite this, ADRO's share price has increased 10.84% since the beginning of the year, and the company has announced a cash dividend distribution of USD 2.62 billion for fiscal year 2023.
title: Foreign investors continue to sell off shares of PT Alamatri Resources Indonesia, a coal mining company, with a net sell of 152.96 million shares in the last 7 days.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) has won the "Best Overall Corporate Governance" award at the 15th IICD Corporate Governance Conference & Award 2024, recognizing its commitment to improving corporate governance practices. This award is based on an assessment of 200 issuers listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, and BNI's consistent implementation of good corporate governance principles is expected to have a positive impact on its business sustainability and credibility.
title: BNI Wins Best Overall Corporate Governance Award for Second Consecutive Year

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.14 billion shares of PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) last week, despite a 3.60% increase in the company's share price to IDR 1,870 per share, with a total of IDR 7 trillion divested from its ice cream business line. The company also announced plans to distribute an interim dividend of IDR 1.56 trillion, or IDR 41 per share, from its net profit collected until the first half of 2024.
title: Foreign investors sell Unilever Indonesia shares despite divestment of ice cream business and dividend announcement.

body: Foreign investors continued to sell off shares of PT Avia Avian TBK (AVIA), a paint manufacturer, with a net sell volume of IDR 115.78 million worth of shares, peaking on November 19, 2024, and a total net sell volume of 45.17 million shares. Despite showing positive growth in revenue and net profit, with a 7.76% year-on-year increase in revenue and a 5.54% year-on-year increase in net profit, Avia's stock price decreased by 12.86% to IDR 420 per share as of last Tuesday's closing bell.
title: Foreign investors continue to sell off shares of Avia Avian, a paint manufacturer, despite its positive revenue and profit growth.
